nodes.name: Blackwater Crossing
nodes.description: A walled frontier town in Belerion, Dragon Isles, at the intersection of the Old Northern Trade Road and the Blackwater River. Built on both river banks, connected by an old stone bridge. The starting point of the Basilisk Campaign.

nodes.name: The Laughing Basilisk
nodes.description: A tavern inside Blackwater Crossing, near the north gate. The party's home base.

nodes.name: The Sweaty Mermaid
nodes.description: A tavern on the east (bad) side of the Blackwater River. Connected to the suspicious meat rumor. Below: stairs to vampire tunnels.

nodes.name: The Twist
nodes.description: A maze of alleys near the Laughing Basilisk. Controlled by a thieves guild ("canting crew").

nodes.name: Tunnels Beneath Blackwater Crossing
nodes.description: Old tunnels under the city. Contains a vampire brothel. Connected to the mystery of the missing children and the fog.

nodes.name: The Cursed Farmhouse
nodes.description: A ruined farmstead along the Old Northern Trade Road, south of Blackwater Crossing. It has a basement. There was a fire here. Something cursed lingers.

nodes.name: The Graveyard
nodes.description: On the Trade Road, 2 hours south of the Old Forest edge. Landmark for navigation to Kalla Cave.

nodes.name: Kalla Cave
nodes.description: 4 hours east of the Graveyard. Contains kalla (food). Mud Tooth Porcs relocated here. A stream runs through this hex toward the cave.

nodes.name: The Mud Wallow
nodes.description: Hot spring west of the Trade Road in the Old Forest. Therapeutic mud heals 1 Heart/hour. Former Mud Tooth Porc territory.

nodes.name: Pjörk Choppe Hille
nodes.description: Organized porc settlement in the Old Forest. ~3/4 day north of BC on the Trade Road, then ~1 hour west.

nodes.name: The Spider Cave
nodes.description: Right behind Pjörk Choppe Hille (~1 hour scramble). Daemon spiders, tunnels to skeletal lord's throne room, Evil Chaos Shrine.

nodes.name: Crag Cairn
nodes.description: SE of Mud Wallow, 2 hours north of Kobold Crevasse. Crag King (barrow wight) on bone throne with 8 skeleton guards. Level 5 encounter.

nodes.name: The Kobold Crevasse
nodes.description: A deep crevasse in the Old Forest east of the Trade Road. Southern entrance in hex 1005, middle in 1105, northern tip in 1205. A path leads down to a kobold settlement. Mystic ring of trees with thaumaturgical runes at the surface. Home to Yip-Snap and her clan.

nodes.name: The Fae Glade
nodes.description: Old-growth forest between Pjörk Choppe Hille and Thornespire Keep. Silver-bark trees with faces. Time differential: minutes inside = hours outside. Also called "The Garden."

nodes.name: Thornespire Keep
nodes.description: Stone fortress on a hilltop in the Old Forest. ~1.5 days from BC (1 day north to forest, 4 hours west). Outer Bailey, Traveler's Inn, Chapel of Muir. The Castellan governs from the Inner Bailey.

nodes.name: Raven's Perch
nodes.description: Abandoned watchtower in the Old Forest, accessed from the Trade Road north of the Thornespire Keep turnoff. Party rescued the Castellan's missing scouts here.

nodes.name: The Swamp
nodes.description: NW of Thornespire Keep, upstream on the Blackwater River. Lizardmen, a hermit who drinks ice wine. Once populated, now flooded.

nodes.name: Mistwood Glen
nodes.description: Mystical sanctuary ~2-3 days SW of BC. The Kalla'din pocket realm. Home to the Thornbeard Circle, Snezynja, and Stone-Nose Clan forest trolls.

nodes.name: Brunhilde's Mountain Watch Camp
nodes.description: Brunhilde Ironfoot's camp & inn on the south road. Waypoint and recruiting camp. Has a moat. Baron Thornwise claims jurisdiction.

nodes.name: Serpent's Pass
nodes.description: Mountain pass where the Trade Road passes through the Serpent's Teeth Crags. ~2 days south of BC.

nodes.name: Bandit Ambush Hill
nodes.description: A rocky hill on the western edge of hex 1113, nearest to hex 1013. Covered in stones and natural cover — an excellent position for a small army to camp, like a Weathertop. The bandits from Hangman Hill used this as an ambush site overlooking the main road. Connected to Hangman Hill's water grotto via underground streambed.

nodes.name: Tower of the Stargazer
nodes.description: Home of Sir Uravulon Calcidius. ~2 days south on Trade Road, half day west. 80-foot stone tower with constant lightning. Calcidius trapped in salt circle ~59 years ago.

nodes.name: Vault of First Light
nodes.description: Ancient dwarven dungeon in the Serpent's Teeth. 2 days south + 1 day overland. Contains traps, the Fallen (undead dwur), cosmic forces.

nodes.name: The North Road
nodes.description: The Old Northern Trade Road exits the known map to the north through hex 1000. The road continues through 1004, 1003, 1002, 1001, 1000 and beyond into unexplored territory.

nodes.name: The South Pass
nodes.description: The Trade Road emerges from the Serpent's Teeth and continues south.

nodes.name: Northern Warding Stone
nodes.description: A carved stone with protective runes in the Old Forest near the Mud Wallow. One of two Vampire Warding Stones that create an invisible fence vampires cannot cross. The warding line runs southwest to its partner at the river.

nodes.name: Southern Warding Stone
nodes.description: A carved stone with protective runes where the Blackwater River enters the Old Forest. One of two Vampire Warding Stones that create an invisible fence vampires cannot cross. The warding line runs northeast to its partner near the Mud Wallow.

nodes.name: The Basilisk Spiderwood
nodes.description: A deep wood in the Old Forest, home to the basilisk (the campaign's namesake creature) and overlapping with giant spider nests. The Mud Tooth Porcs call the basilisk's clearing 'the quiet ground'. Petrified stone figures have been found in the spider webs — the basilisk was there before the spiders.
